text: Amazon faces material financial risks related to capital intensity and free cash flow volatility. Capital expenditure reached $32.183 billion in Q2 2025, nearly matching operating cash flow of $32.515 billion for the period, resulting in free cash flow of only $332 million or a 0.20% FCF margin. This represents a significant improvement from Q1 2025, when free cash flow was negative $8.004 billion, indicating substantial quarter-to-quarter variability in cash generation. Total debt stands at $133.939 billion as of Q2 2025, with a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.40, suggesting moderate leverage. The current ratio of 1.02 indicates tight liquidity positioning, with current assets of $191.420 billion barely exceeding current liabilities of $186.921 billion.
